items.title: Cruz Hewitt beaten by American prospect Jordan Lee in Wimbledon boys' final
items.summary: Cruz Hewitt comes up short in his bid to win the Wimbledon boys' title, being beaten by Jordan Lee in the final.

items.title: Brendon McCullum fired as England's Test coach but retains white ball duties
items.summary: The architect of England's controversial Bazball approach to Test cricket is given his marching orders after a disappointing string of results.

items.title: Convicted paedophile argues multiple-personality evidence unlawful in appeal
items.summary: A father jailed for abusing two children is appealing against his conviction, arguing evidence given by the multiple personalities of the same witness was unlawful.

items.title: Noosa Council and Queensland Police crack down on illegal camping
items.summary: A crackdown on illegal camping in Noosa, prompted by hundreds of complaints, has sparked outrage locally, with many arguing fines will not solve the issue.

items.title: SA doctor banned for inappropriate relationship with patient who accused him of 'grooming' her
items.summary: A former Adelaide doctor will not be able to reapply for registration for at least two years after he was banned for having an inappropriate relationship with his client.

items.title: Jannik Sinner beats Alexander Zverev to win Wimbledon again
items.summary: The top-ranked Jannik Sinner beat Alexander Zverev for his second consecutive title at Wimbledon after his German opponent appeared bothered by a knee issue.

items.title: Report says growing Sydney's tree canopy would save $1 billion in health costs
items.summary: A series of greenways crisscrossing Sydney's west presents an opportunity to grow the city's canopy and make more public space, a Committee for Sydney report suggests.

items.title: As swift parrots kept breeding in this forest, the government realised it had a problem
items.summary: It was late 2021 when one of Australia's rarest birds started to arrive in the native forests of Lonnavale. Ecologists had expected it but for a reacting forestry sector, it was "not sustainable".

items.title: Labor scoring own goals while Coalition distracted by One Nation threat
items.summary: When the bells resume ringing in the halls of parliament, Anthony Albanese and Angus Taylor will both do well to dispose of any complacency.

items.title: With AI, we have returned to a world of two competing superpowers
items.summary: When it comes to AI, Australia is a spectator at a UFC cage fight between the United States and China, except the spectators are inside the cage with the combatants.

items.title: Four Corners analysed 'affordable' rental listings. We found a reality that didn't match the promise
items.summary: Governments across Australia are spending billions to boost the number of rentals available to people on low and middle incomes. A Four Corners investigation found many of these homes were unaffordable for those who needed them most, particularly single-income households.

items.title: Small and achievable ways to drink less alcohol
items.summary: If you’ve fallen off the Dry July wagon or you just aren’t interested in teetotalling, trying smaller steps to reducing your alcohol intake can still make a difference to your health.

items.title: Universities required to define antisemitism under new racism standards
items.summary: The new standards will require institutions to adopt definitions of antisemitism, racism against Aboriginal and Torres Straight Islander peoples, and Islamophobia.

items.title: Pharmacy lobby preventing Australians from buying cheaper medicines, Grattan Institute says
items.summary: Taxpayers are missing out on cheaper medicines due to backroom deals by one of Australia's most powerful lobby groups, according to a new report.

items.title: Jai Arrow vows to fight MND for everyone during 'World's Biggest Birthday Party'
items.summary: Former NRL player Jai Arrow says he is determined to raise money and awareness for Motor Neurone Disease until a cure is found.

items.title: Can the pill affect your mental health? Young woman says mood swings were too much to bear
items.summary: Two young women say taking the pill to manage painful periods had side effects they weren't expecting.

items.title: World Cup: Bellingham goes big time again, as England continues down hard road
items.summary: Jude Bellingham has been scrutinised and criticised in the months leading up to the World Cup. Now he stands as England's best player so far, carrying the Three Lions' title dreams on his shoulders.

items.title: World Cup controversy as Norway fumes over England's spidercam-assisted equaliser
items.summary: Norway felt England's equaliser shouldn't have stood after the ball appeared to get a deflection off a wire holding up the "spidercam" above the pitch, directly leading to England gaining possession and scoring.

items.title: Love story: Motherhood is helping Moana Hope re-learn how to love
items.summary: The former AFLW player was an adult when she realised her childhood had warped her understanding of love.

items.title: China ballistic missile test pushes forward case for Pacific Security Agreement
items.summary: Pacific Minister Pat Conroy says the testing of a nuclear-capable missile damaged China's reputation and has strengthened the case for a Pacific security pact.

items.title: Numbat conservation efforts pull native species from brink of extinction
items.summary: Numbats, Western Australia's animal emblem, have been pulled from the brink of extinction after research found the population was growing in the wild.

items.title: Horsham footballer 'alert' in Melbourne hospital after on-field head knock
items.summary: A young regional footballer who sustained a head knock and concussion during a match on Saturday is recovering well in a Melbourne hospital, according to his club.

items.title: How a fireman helped make dinosaur fossils cool again
items.summary: Travis Enright is four months into investigating what he calls "a 100-million-year-old cold case" involving a 10-metre-long aquatic dinosaur called Moria.

items.title: Ensay's football homecoming revives decades-old legacy
items.summary: A special tribute match at Ensay's oval in regional Victoria resurrected a football tradition and brought generations of a Gippsland high country community together.

items.title: Invisible string that Australia attached to PNG's rugby league team
items.summary: In a contest for Pacific influence against China, Australia's football diplomacy comes with risks and rewards.

items.title: South African World Cup player Jayden Adams dies aged 25
items.summary: Jayden Adams, who made three appearances for South Africa at the World Cup, has died aged 25.

items.title: Linda Nosková survives Karolína Muchová fightback in all-Czech final to lift Wimbledon women's singles trophy
items.summary: Linda Nosková becomes the third Czech woman in four years to claim the Wimbledon singles title as she downs her great friend in the final.

items.title: Australia's first map of city zoning shows Melbourne streets ahead on density
items.summary: Four years on from a national pledge to unlock more housing, only one capital city allows townhouses or three-storey developments on most of its residential land.

items.title: Can Australia avoid the next round of the global energy crisis?
items.summary: As the conflict has dragged on, gas prices have soared and now threaten to up-end the economies of Europe and Asia.

items.title: Speedboat capsize off Vietnam's Phu Quoc island kills 15 tourists
items.summary: A tourist boat returning from an island trip in southern Vietnam has capsized, killing 15 Indian visitors, state media has reported.

items.title: France dominates Wallabies to post historic Test win
items.summary: Following a second-half blitz, France scores its fourth straight victory over the Wallabies, triumphing 42-26 in the Nations Championship Test in Brisbane.

items.title: Carbon monoxide poisoning suspected in fatal Mount Gambier incident
items.summary: A 55-year-old has died and three other men have been taken to hospital after a case of suspected carbon monoxide poisoning at a Mount Gambier home in South Australia's south-east.

items.title: Hepburn Shire mayor says charges in 'private prosecution' by resident a 'threat to democracy'
items.summary: The mayor of Hepburn Shire in Victoria is the third council representative to be charged by the same resident via a rare "private prosecution".

items.title: A warming veggie and lentil soup that's ready in less than 30 minutes - ABC News
items.summary: This easy, healthy, pantry-based soup is a saviour if your fridge is looking bare.

items.title: Remembering the quadriplegic artist who drew the outback without using his hands
items.summary: Left quadriplegic in an accident, every line the late Gunggari man drew and every word he scrawled in ink was created without a pen in his hands.

items.title: Broken Hill mother exposed to lead as a child fights same battle with her children
items.summary: Baylee Stokes lives with the health impacts of being exposed to lead as a child. Now, she's considering moving away from her hometown to protect her own children.

items.title: Living in the 'forgotten' community of Beagle Bay
items.summary: Residents of the Indigenous community of Beagle Bay say they feel forgotten by their landlord: the Western Australian government.

items.title: H5 bird flu poses risk to SA's Coorong critical wetlands
items.summary: South Australia's Coorong is a sanctuary for birds and marine life but it is increasingly under threat with the arrival of bird flu in Australia the latest in a rolling barrage of crises.

items.title: Handmade quilts weave culture into Forster NAIDOC Week baby celebration
items.summary: Beautiful Indigenous-themed quilts have been made by a group of women for a NAIDOC Week baby celebration on Worimi country, linking cultures and generations.

items.title: Curbing well-off taxpayers' access to key programs could save billions
items.summary: Curbing access to the child care subsidy, parental leave pay, aged care and pensions for some of the most well-off taxpayers would free up about $21 billion a year, a new report says.

items.title: The Interview: Jessica Mauboy wants to share the culture and community of her home
items.summary: From singing to her daughter to laughing with her aunties, Jessica Mauboy is all about family. This NAIDOC Week, the star is sharing the music, culture and community she grew up with in a whole new way.

items.title: The battle for the Blue Zones
items.summary: The Blue Zones grew into a global wellness brand aided by stories of legendary old age. But a bitter feud over the science has shaken their foundations.

items.title: Bayeux Tapestry returns to Britain for first time in nearly 1,000 years
items.summary: The Bayeux Tapestry has arrived in Britain for the first time in nearly 1,000 years, travelling under police escort during a meticulously choreographed journey from France.

items.title: China achieves first-ever controlled rocket recovery
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: China achieves first-ever controlled recovery of orbital-class rocket
items.summary: China has successfully tested an experimental rocket retrieval system, state media reported, in the hope of breaking US dominance in reusable rockets.

items.title: Tributes for Derryn Hinch after his death in Melbourne at the age of 82
items.summary: Media identity Derryn Hinch is being remembered as a provocative but courageous journalist who was ahead of his time in his fight against institutional sex abuse.

items.title: Wallabies captain says recent results not good enough ahead of Test match against France
items.summary: Close enough will not be good enough for the Wallabies, even with an untried flyhalf and familiar French pack ready to inflict a sixth-straight Test defeat on the hosts in Brisbane.

items.title: Lush native lawn ideas for waterwise, lower-maintenance gardens
items.summary: If mowing the lawn is getting you down, it might be time to ditch your exotic buffalo, couch or kikuyu grass and switch to a native alternative.

items.title: Coffee Camp cattle farmer plants 14,000 trees for flood mitigation
items.summary: Jeff Imeson has planted around 14,000 trees on his property near Nimbin in a bid to help slow the flow of future floods.

items.title: World Cup: Whether in tuxedos or workwear, France can win in any fashion
items.summary: France has been peerless at this World Cup. And as their superstars continue to deliver on demand, Les Bleus are proving they can win no matter the circumstances.

items.title: The Rolling Stones — Foreign Tongues review: a late-career peak in their eighties
items.summary: On album #25, Foreign Tongues, rock's elder statesmen once again dismantle concerns whether men in their eighties can still make great records.

items.title: BBL to play first overseas match in India as Scorchers face Renegades
items.summary: The historic BBL game in India, between Melbourne Renegades and Perth Scorchers, is every chance to be a one-off spectacular, with officials reluctant to commit beyond 2026.

items.title: Why potatoes can be the star of the show and what types to use
items.summary: Potatoes are undeniably versatile and tasty but choosing which variety to buy has never been more complicated. Brushed or washed? Yellow, white or pink? Mashing or roasting? And now there are low-carb spuds too.

items.title: Ilfracombe's Machinery Mile grows as 20th century tractors arrive from state museum
items.summary: Ilfracombe's roadside museum is almost as long as the town itself and enshrines machines from Australia's past. Locals are preparing to display the latest donation of four historic tractors.

items.title: Environmental groups say Victoria's state forests being logged 'by stealth'
items.summary: In the wake of the Victorian government's move away from native timber harvesting, environmental groups are calling for greater management of timber by-product removals in state forests.

items.title: Australian War Memorial makes 'magnificent' discovery of first Indigenous Military Cross recipient
items.summary: Captain Sidney Leonard Morse's achievements would have remained hidden were it not for a chance discovery by a volunteer historian collating family trees at the Australian War Memorial.

items.title: Aussie Rules club Fregon Bulldogs' new guernseys bring change in fortunes
items.summary: The Fregon Bulldogs are among the nation's most remote Australian Rules football clubs, and for several seasons were forced to play in casual clothes. But the club has secured new guernseys — and the change in uniform has led to a change in form.
